bsiTr03161EinfachErklaertDetail.title: BSI TR-03161 simply explained: What DiGA manufacturers need to know now
bsiTr03161EinfachErklaertDetail.description: BSI TR-03161 has been mandatory for DiGA manufacturers since 2025.Find out what the policy means, what three parts it consists of and what happens if you don't meet the requirements.
bsiTr03161EinfachErklaertDetail.author: Kerim K.

introduction.title: What is BSI TR-03161?
introduction.content: The BSI Technical Guideline TR-03161 was developed by the Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) and defines binding security requirements for digital health applications (DiGA).It specifies which technical security measures manufacturers must implement so that their application can be included in the DiGA directory of the Federal Institute for Drugs and Medical Devices (BfArM).The guideline is divided into three parts: Part 1 for mobile applications, Part 2 for web applications and Part 3 for background systems (backends and APIs).It is based significantly on established OWASP standards - in particular the OWASP MASVS (Mobile Application Security Verification Standard) and the OWASP ASVS (Application Security Verification Standard).

keyTakeaway.title: Key message
keyTakeaway.content: BSI TR-03161 is the binding security guideline for DiGA and DiPA.Without proof of conformity, no inclusion in the DiGA directory and no reimbursement by health insurance companies.

introduction.quote: The TR-03161 is not a recommendation – it is the ticket to the DiGA directory.

commonAttackVectors.title: The three parts of TR-03161
items: Part 1 – Mobile Applications: Security Requirements for Native and Hybrid Mobile Apps.Testing aspects include data storage, cryptography, authentication, network communication, platform interaction and code quality - based on OWASP MASVS and the Mobile Security Testing Guide (MASTG).

Part 2 – Web Applications: Requirements for Browser-Based Healthcare Applications.Focus on authentication, session management, input validation, content security policy and secure communication – based on OWASP ASVS.
Part 3 – Background Systems: Security requirements for backend systems, APIs and databases.Testing aspects include authorization, data storage, cryptography, secure configuration and logging.
Threat Scenarios: Each part defines specific threat scenarios that must be considered during security testing.These are based on real attack patterns in the healthcare system.
Testing aspects: The guideline describes specific test aspects that an auditor must evaluate during certification - from secure key storage to resistance to reverse engineering.

impactAnalysis.title: Why is this a legal requirement from 2025?
impactAnalysis.content: The regulatory basis is the Digital Health Applications Ordinance (DiGAV) in conjunction with Section 139e SGB V. Since 2025, DiGA manufacturers have had to demonstrate conformity with BSI TR-03161 in order to be included in the DiGA directory.Without this inclusion, reimbursement by statutory health insurance companies is not possible.

items.label: No inclusion in the DiGA directory
No reimbursement by health insurance companies
Repair costs after a rejected test

protectionStrategies.title: What does this mean specifically for manufacturers?
protectionStrategies.content: As a DiGA manufacturer, you must ensure that your application meets the testing aspects of TR-03161 - for all components: mobile app, web frontend and backend.The official certification is carried out by a testing center recognized by the BSI.A preparatory security check by a specialized provider such as Sodu Secure identifies vulnerabilities early on and saves time and money.

strategies.title: Perform gap analysis
strategies.description: Have an early check to see where your application already meets the requirements of TR-03161 and where there is still a need for action.

strategies.title: Security by design
strategies.description: Integrate the security requirements of TR-03161 into the development phase - not just before certification.

strategies.title: Carry out preparatory pentest
strategies.description: A penetration test according to the testing aspects of TR-03161 reveals technical vulnerabilities before the official testing center finds them.

strategies.title: Prepare documentation
strategies.description: Certification requires extensive documentation.Prepare security concepts, risk analyzes and test reports early on.

conclusion.title: Conclusion: Preparing early pays off
conclusion.content: BSI TR-03161 is not a bureaucratic hurdle, but rather a sensible security standard for applications that work with sensitive health data.Those who prepare early save costs and time when it comes to official certification.

tr03161ZertifizierungVorbereitungDetail.author: Kerim K.

introduction.title: The path to TR-03161 certification
introduction.content: Certification according to BSI TR-03161 has been mandatory for DiGA manufacturers since 2025.But the way there doesn't have to be complicated if you approach it in a structured manner.In this article we will show you the specific steps, typical stumbling blocks and why a preparatory security check can make the difference.The certification is carried out by a testing center recognized by the BSI.But before you knock there, you should make sure that your application actually meets the testing aspects of TR-03161.This is exactly where we start.

keyTakeaway.title: Important to know
keyTakeaway.content: Thorough preparation reduces the risk of failing the official certification – thereby saving time, money and stress.

introduction.quote: The best preparation for an exam is to have passed it before - under realistic conditions.

commonAttackVectors.title: The steps to certification
items: Step 1 – Understand Requirements: Familiarize yourself with the three parts of TR-03161.Identify which parts are relevant to your application (mobile, web, backend, or all three).
Step 2 – Internal inventory: Check internally which security measures have already been implemented.Document the current status systematically.

Step 3 – Gap Analysis: Have a professional gap analysis carried out.A specialized service provider compares your current status with the requirements of TR-03161 and shows specifically where action is required.
Step 4 – Fix vulnerabilities: Work on the identified gaps.Prioritize by criticality – critical findings first.
Step 5 – Preparatory pen test: Have a penetration test carried out according to the testing aspects of TR-03161.This allows you to identify weak points that were not visible in the gap analysis.
Step 6 - Prepare documentation: The official testing center requires extensive documentation: security concept, risk analysis, test reports, architecture documentation.
Step 7 – Official certification: Commission a testing center recognized by the BSI to carry out the official certification.With good preparation you will succeed on the first attempt.

impactAnalysis.title: Typical stumbling blocks
impactAnalysis.content: From our experience with healthcare applications, we know: There are recurring problems that surprise manufacturers when preparing TR-03161.

protectionStrategies.title: Why a preparatory pen test makes sense
protectionStrategies.content: A penetration test before official certification is not mandatory - but it is one of the most sensible investments you can make.It simulates the test situation under realistic conditions.

strategies.title: Find vulnerabilities before the audit
strategies.description: A pen test uncovers technical vulnerabilities that are not visible in a document-based gap analysis.

strategies.title: Save costs
strategies.description: A failed certification audit not only costs the repeat fee, but also valuable time until the test is repeated.

strategies.title: Deliver concrete fixes to developers
strategies.description: Our test report not only contains findings, but also concrete recommendations for action and code level fixes.

mobileAppSecurityGesundheitswesenDetail.title: Mobile app security in healthcare: The most common vulnerabilities according to BSI TR-03161
mobileAppSecurityGesundheitswesenDetail.description: Technical overview of the most common security problems in mobile health applications - and how testing according to BSI TR-03161 helps to identify them.
mobileAppSecurityGesundheitswesenDetail.author: Kerim K.

introduction.title: Why mobile app security is particularly critical in healthcare
introduction.content: Mobile health applications process particularly sensitive data: diagnoses, treatment courses, medication plans and personal health information.According to GDPR Art. 9, these data belong to the special categories of personal data and are subject to increased protection requirements.BSI TR-03161 Part 1 addresses exactly these risks.It defines security requirements based on the OWASP Mobile Application Security Verification Standard (MASVS) and the OWASP Mobile Application Security Testing Guide (MASTG).In our practice as security auditors, we see recurring vulnerabilities in healthcare applications, which we present in this article.

keyTakeaway.title: Increased need for protection
keyTakeaway.content: Health data is one of the special categories according to GDPR Art. 9. A security gap can have not only financial consequences, but also health consequences.

introduction.quote: Health data isn’t just about data protection – it’s about patient trust in digital medicine.

commonAttackVectors.title: The most common vulnerabilities according to TR-03161 Part 1
items: Insecure local data storage: Health data is stored unencrypted in Shared Preferences (Android) or the file system.SQLite databases without encryption-at-rest are also a common finding.TR-03161 explicitly requires the encryption of sensitive data on the device.

Missing or incorrect certificate pinning: Without certificate pinning, an attacker can use a manipulated certificate to eavesdrop on the TLS connection between the app and the backend (man-in-the-middle attack).Particularly critical when health data is transferred.

Insufficient authentication: weak password policies, lack of multi-factor procedures or insecure biometric implementations.TR-03161 requires robust authentication mechanisms that meet the data protection needs.
Sensitive data in logs and backups: Debug logs containing health data can be read via ADB (Android) or iTunes backups (iOS).Screenshots of the app can also reveal sensitive information.
Insecure API communication: Missing authorization checks on API endpoints, IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerabilities that allow access to other users' health data.
Weak cryptography implementations: use of outdated algorithms (MD5, SHA1), hard-coded keys in the source code, insecure random number generation or faulty key derivation functions.

Lack of resilience against reverse engineering: Without code obfuscation, root/jailbreak detection and tampering protection, an attacker can analyze the app, manipulate it and circumvent security mechanisms.
Insecure Inter-Process Communication: Exported activities, content providers without access restrictions or insecure deep links can be abused by other apps on the device.

impactAnalysis.title: Impact of these vulnerabilities
impactAnalysis.content: The consequences of a security breach in a healthcare application go beyond a normal data protection incident.In addition to the regulatory consequences (fines according to the GDPR, exclusion from the DiGA directory), there is a risk that patients will lose trust in digital healthcare.

protectionStrategies.title: How we help: Testing according to TR-03161 standards
protectionStrategies.content: At Sodu Secure, we carry out security tests that are based on the test aspects and threat scenarios of BSI TR-03161.Our methodology includes:

strategies.title: Static Analysis (SAST)
strategies.description: Analyzing the source code or compiled app for hardcoded keys, insecure API calls, cryptography weaknesses and configuration errors.

strategies.title: Dynamic Analysis (DAST)
strategies.description: Runtime analysis of the app: network traffic, data storage, IPC communication, authentication flows and session handling.

strategies.title: Manual penetration test
strategies.description: Targeted attack simulation by experienced pen testers: Exploitation of identified vulnerabilities with proof of concept.

strategies.title: Detailed test report
strategies.description: All findings with CVSS evaluation, reproduction steps, screenshots and concrete recommendations for action for your developers.

conclusion.title: Conclusion: Early testing protects patients and your company
conclusion.content: The most common vulnerabilities in mobile health applications are known and preventable.An early security test according to the standards of BSI TR-03161 identifies these problems before they become apparent during official certification or - worse still - in productive operation.
